mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive 20d returns occur in < 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes yield positive 5d returns in > 55% of cases during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth confirms robust economic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive 60d returns occur in < 50% of high GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 235.0 indicates persistent inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate demonstrates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to predict positive 20d returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and broader demand weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST signals yield positive 5d returns in > 55% of cases during post-shock recovery phases.
